text.title: Trunk-Based Challenges & Diagnostics Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Postmortem after the third trunk break of the week concludes 'trunk-based development is wrong for us; let's go back to GitFlow'.
antipattern.root-cause: Team treats trunk-based development as the cause; ignores missing gate (CI speed, branch protection, flags).
antipattern.fix: Run the ordered checklist. Fix the first failing gate. Re-evaluate after 2 weeks before any structural change to branching.

antipattern.symptom: Report lists 17 actions; team prioritises by gut; nothing improves in 6 weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: Failed to apply ordered checklist; treated all symptoms equally.
antipattern.fix: Identify ONE first failing check. Ship its fix. Re-measure. Only then move to the next.

antipattern.symptom: All PRs touching domain X queue behind Alice; PRs age 2 days waiting.
antipattern.root-cause: Single-reviewer policy by accident, not design.
antipattern.fix: Train a second reviewer; rotate primary-reviewer monthly; add CODEOWNERS with at least 2 entries per path.

antipattern.symptom: Developer opens a 9-day branch claiming 'feature is too big to chunk'.
antipattern.root-cause: Task decomposition not applied; BbA available but not used.
antipattern.fix: Apply trunk-based-branch-by-abstraction; split the work into trunk-merged increments behind a flag.

antipattern.symptom: Audit finds 47 active flags; no one knows what 12 of them gate; old flags still being evaluated at runtime.
antipattern.root-cause: No flag inventory; cleanup tickets never filed.
antipattern.fix: Build .aidocs/feature-flags.md as the canonical inventory; require task-id + cleanup-ticket per flag; review weekly until backlog < 10 stale flags.
